original_text:
Time: 2:00 AM on June 16, 2026, Vietnam time.

Tournament: Group G - 2026 World Cup Finals.

Live football streaming links: VTV3, VTV6, VTV9, VTV10, FPTPlay, TV360.

The referee allowed Rami Rabia to come on after Emam Ashour left the field.

The match was attended by 66,775 spectators.

Due to the hot weather, the referee allowed players from both teams to take a break to rehydrate.

Mohamed Hany inadvertently put the opponent's ball into his own net. The score became 1-1.

Romelu Lukaku came on as a substitute for Charles De Ketelaere.

Mohamed Salah didn't cross the ball into the box, but instead made a short pass from the corner kick.

Omar Marmoush unleashed a powerful shot, but the Belgian defender blocked it with his body. Egypt were awarded a corner kick.

Kevin De Bruyne took the corner kick with a short pass.

Kevin De Bruyne received the ball inside the penalty area, but his shot towards the center of the goal was safely saved by the goalkeeper.

Youri Tielemans volleyed from the edge of the penalty area, the ball narrowly missing the left post.

Kevin De Bruyne delivered a cross from the corner, but the opposing defender rose high to clear the ball.

Jeremy Doku sprinted towards the goal, but the defender reacted quickly to intercept.

Omar Marmoush made a skillful run into the penalty area, but his decisive shot went over the crossbar.

Youri Tielemans played a through pass to Kevin De Bruyne, but the pass was intercepted by an opposing defender.

Amadou Onana leaves the field, Maxim De Cuyper comes on as a substitute.

Nicolas Raskin was brought on by Rudi Garcia to replace Timothy Castagne.

Mohamed Salah rose high to head the ball towards the right post, but the goalkeeper made an excellent save. Emam Ashour then followed up with a shot but sent the ball wide.

Omar Marmoush tried to break free from the defenders but couldn't get past them.

Mohamed Salah delivered a dangerous cross from the corner, but the opposing defense prevented the Egyptian players from jumping to receive the ball.

Mostafa Ziko controlled Mohamed Salah's cross well, but the defender intervened in time. Egypt were awarded a corner kick.

Kevin De Bruyne took a beautiful free kick, but the ball hit the left post.

Marwan Attia intercepted the ball but committed a foul, giving Belgium a free kick that could have created a scoring opportunity.

Jeremy Doku passed the ball into the penalty area, but the opposing defender managed to clear the danger.

Omar Marmoush committed a foul and Belgium were awarded a free kick.

Mostafa Ziko took a shot inside the penalty area but it was blocked by a defender. Omar Marmoush followed up with a shot but it was also blocked.

Leandro Trossard threw the ball in quickly, allowing his teammate to sprint into the opponent's penalty area.

The match is ready to enter the second half.

The first 45 minutes ended after the referee's whistle.

The defense cleared the ball safely after a dangerous cross from Mohamed Salah's corner kick.

Mohamed Hany took a long-range shot, but the defenders blocked his attempt.

Emam Ashour quickly took the corner kick to increase the pace of the game.

Omar Marmoush decided to make a short pass from the corner kick.

Omar Marmoush took a shot towards the bottom left corner after a scramble, but the shot was too weak and Thibaut Courtois easily caught it.

Thomas Meunier committed a foul and referee Ramon Abatti awarded the opposing team a free kick.

Jeremy Doku took a one-touch shot inside the penalty area, but the ball went wide of the right post.

The match had 4 minutes of added time in the first half.

Leandro Trossard unleashed a volley from the edge of the penalty area, but the Belgian player's shot was blocked by an opposing defender.

The Belgian players consistently used short passes to retain possession while simultaneously looking for opportunities to break through the Egyptian defense.

Youri Tielemans made a rather rough tackle and referee Ramon Abatti blew the whistle for a foul.

Ahmed Fatouh committed a foul and received a yellow card from referee Ramon Abatti.

From the corner kick, Emam Ashour didn't cross the ball into the penalty area but opted for a short pass instead.

Marwan Attia unleashed a powerful shot towards the left side of the goal, forcing the goalkeeper to make an excellent save. The ball went wide, and Egypt were awarded a corner kick.

The total number of shots is currently 3:1, while the number of shots on target so far is 0:1.

Timothy Castagne delivered a good cross to Youri Tielemans, but the Belgian's header from near the penalty spot went wide.

Youri Tielemans sent the ball into a dangerous area, but the opposing defense managed to clear it in time.

Thomas Meunier committed a bad foul and the referee immediately blew the whistle.

Kevin De Bruyne delivered a skillful lob pass to Thomas Meunier. The latter controlled the ball cleanly but couldn't get past the opposing defenders.

De Bruyne chipped the ball delicately to Thomas Meunier. The player controlled it well with his first touch but was then stopped by the Egyptian defender and lost possession.

Due to the high temperature outside the field, the referee decided to give both teams a break to rehydrate.

Brandon Mechele was penalized for a foul after grabbing his opponent's shirt and holding him down.

From a pass by Mohamed Salah, Emam Ashour controlled the ball neatly outside the penalty area before unleashing a powerful shot into the bottom left corner, giving Egypt a 1-0 lead.

Kevin De Bruyne delivered a well-placed through pass, nearly creating a dangerous opportunity for Belgium if the Egyptian defense hadn't intervened in time.

The ball possession statistics currently stand at 43:57, favoring one of the two teams.

Kevin De Bruyne executed a well-calculated throw-in, giving Belgium an opportunity to launch a counter-attack.

Ahmed Fatouh sent a long ball to Omar Marmoush, but Thibaut Courtois read the situation well and rushed out to gather the ball.

Timothy Castagne's tackle was too aggressive, prompting referee Ramon Abatti to issue a yellow card.

Marwan Attia received a yellow card after an incident that caused a disruption to the match.

Mohamed Salah passed the ball to Mostafa Ziko, but the latter was unable to get past the opposing defender.

Charles De Ketelaere broke through and passed the ball into the penalty area, but Leandro Trossard couldn't retain possession under pressure from the Egyptian defender.

Mohamed Salah's foul did not escape referee Ramon Abatti's attention, and the game was stopped.

De Bruyne unleashed a low shot from outside the penalty area after a well-executed one-two pass, but the ball went wide of the Egyptian goalpost.

Both Belgium and Egypt entered the match with an aggressive approach, making no secret of their goal to put pressure on the opponent's half.

The whistle of referee Ramon Abatti Abel blew, and the match began.

The Belgium vs Egypt match in Group G of the 2026 World Cup is a notable clash between a star-studded European team and an African side boasting a disciplined defense. Belgium is considered slightly superior due to its squad quality, experience in major tournaments, and ability to create breakthroughs from multiple positions.

Kevin De Bruyne remains central to Belgium's game plan. His passing ability, wide play, and ability to create chances give the Red Devils numerous options for attacking Egypt. Up front, Lukaku provides strength in the penalty area, while Doku can make a difference with his speedy dribbling.

Egypt entered the match with great confidence after a successful qualifying campaign. Hossam Hassan's team played pragmatically, focusing on defense and always waiting for opportunities to switch tactics. Salah and Marmoush were two key players who could make the Belgian defense pay the price if they left any gaps.

The outcome of the match could be decided in the midfield. Belgium wants to dictate the tempo with their ball control, while Egypt needs to maintain a tight formation to limit space in front of the penalty area. This is unlikely to be a high-scoring match.
